title: helianthemum nummularium subsp. urrielense
intro: Helianthemum nummularium subsp. urrielense, also known as rockrose, is a plant species in the family Cistaceae, native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is said to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Rockrose can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for a range of applications, including topical treatments and internal use under medical guidance.
